title: Operation ID idempotency (op-result store + replay)
acceptance_criteria: domain.OpResultStore port: Get(opID) (raw json, ok, err), Put(opID, payload) error. Adapter persists results at .tasks/operations/results/<op-id>.json (gitignored), atomic write via tmp+rename, max payload size 1 MiB.

Every mutating verb (add, update, claim, release, heartbeat, done, journal, move, rename, delete, clone, import) wraps its work in WithIdempotency(opID, fn) which: (a) returns the persisted result if Get(opID) succeeds, (b) runs fn and persists fn's result via Put(opID, ...) on success.
Events.jsonl still records op_id for audit (§6 Layer 7) but is no longer the source of replay payloads — the op-result store is.
Auto-mints op_id when absent and surfaces it on the response so retries can pass it back.
TTL: op-result entries older than 24h are pruned by `task compact` (Phase 8); the table notes this so the dir doesn't grow unboundedly.
Tests cover: first call writes a result and returns it; second call with same op_id returns the persisted result without mutating state; result for a missing op_id returns Get's not-found path.
